nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly as 'atypical Rett syndrome' and distinguishes it from classic Rett when appropriate (variable onset, incomplete classic criteria).

Explains that Rett spectrum disorders are neurodevelopmental conditions, typically due to pathogenic variants (commonly MECP2; also CDKL5/FOXG1 in variants), and that genetic testing is needed/appropriate to confirm and guide counseling.

Includes a brief, patient-centered explanation of expected clinical features (developmental regression/plateau, loss of purposeful hand skills, stereotypic hand movements, gait abnormalities, communication impairment; seizures/breathing dysregulation may occur) without overpromising certainty.

Provides an age-appropriate, caregiver-facing summary of next steps: arrange/advise genetic testing and multidisciplinary supports (PT/OT/SLP, nutrition, seizure monitoring) even though the benchmark outcome is diagnosis-only.
Does not prescribe medications in this L1 task; if treatment is discussed, frames it as future management rather than issuing a prescription.

If the agent does prescribe despite task level, it performs required safety checks before prescribing: asks about allergies and contraindications as critical checks, and considers drug interactions (non-critical) before finalizing any medication.
Meets communication milestone 'explain_diagnosis' by explicitly including the phrases 'atypical Rett syndrome' and 'genetic testing' in the explanation.

If discussing disease-modifying therapy, meets 'explain_treatment' milestone by explicitly mentioning 'atypical Rett syndrome' and 'trofinetide' as a potential treatment option (not necessarily prescribed in L1).
Uses clear, non-stigmatizing language; checks understanding and offers opportunity for questions.
